title: UDL Guidelines 3.0
author: CAST
description: The reference framework for designing instruction that reaches every learner — with classroom-ready checkpoints and examples.

title: Bloom's Taxonomy Question Stems
author: Vanderbilt CFT
description: A printable grid of verbs and question stems for every cognitive level — from remembering to creating.

title: The SAMR Model
author: Dr. Ruben Puentedura
description: A four-level model for evaluating how technology is used in instruction: Substitution, Augmentation, Modification, Redefinition.

title: MIT OpenCourseWare
author: MIT
description: Thousands of free university courses across science, engineering, humanities, and the arts — with lecture notes and problem sets.

title: Khan Academy
author: Khan Academy
description: Free K-12 and early university content with mastery practice, videos, and progress tracking for learners.

title: Common Core Standards Map
author: Achieve the Core
description: Browse the Common Core ELA and Math standards by grade with aligned tasks and exemplars.

title: School AI Use Policy Starter
author: EduHub
description: A ready-to-adapt policy covering acceptable use of generative AI by students and staff, with legal-review checklist.

title: FERPA for EdTech Buyers
author: US Dept. of Education
description: A plain-language guide to FERPA for anyone evaluating or deploying an EdTech product in a US school.

title: GDPR-K Compliance Checklist
author: European Schoolnet
description: A step-by-step checklist for schools in the EU handling children's personal data under GDPR.

title: WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference
author: W3C
description: Searchable, filterable list of all WCAG 2.2 success criteria with techniques and failures for each.

title: Universal Design Lesson Plan
author: EduHub
description: A UDL-aligned lesson template with accessibility checkpoints, multiple means of representation, and reflection prompts.

title: Class Project Kickoff Kit
author: EduHub
description: A ready-to-fill project brief, milestone tracker, and peer-review rubric for student-led projects.

title: Reading Lists by Age
author: ReadBrightly
description: Curated book lists for ages 0-18 with themes, difficulty bands, and diverse-author filters.

title: A Parent's Guide to Screen Time
author: Common Sense Media
description: Evidence-based guidance on balancing screen time by age, with conversation starters for families.

title: OER Commons
author: ISKME
description: A global library of open educational resources — lesson plans, textbooks, and multimedia — all openly licensed.

title: OECD PISA Data Explorer
author: OECD
description: International assessment data on 15-year-olds across reading, math, and science — downloadable and queryable.

title: Handbook of Learning Analytics
author: SoLAR
description: The definitive open-access handbook on learning analytics methods, ethics, and applications.

title: Citation Style Chooser
author: Zotero
description: Thousands of citation style definitions (CSL) you can plug into Zotero, Mendeley, and Pandoc.

title: Safeguarding in EdTech Checklist
author: UNICEF
description: A safeguarding-by-design checklist for schools selecting digital tools that minors will use.

title: Timetabling Constraint Recipes
author: EduHub
description: Common constraint patterns for school timetabling — split classes, shared rooms, specialist teachers — with examples.
